title: Agentic AI: the future of space warfare
summary: The future of space warfare will rely on the speed of decision-making as satellite constellations and counterspace capabilities advance. Agentic AI, a form of artificial intelligence, is poised to enhance military decision-making in this domain.

title: Antaris™ Raises $28 Million Series A to Accelerate AI-Driven Space Missions
summary: Antaris raised $28 million in Series A funding to advance its AI-driven platform for satellite missions. The company's platform simplifies satellite design, simulation, manufacturing, and operations for ISR and communications satellites.

title: China targets 140 launches in 2026 amid commercial space surge
summary: China plans to conduct around 140 orbital launches in 2026, marking a significant increase in its launch activities amid a surge in commercial space operations.

title: Europe’s space sector faces power shift as funding grows
summary: Europe's space sector is experiencing a power shift due to increased funding and a growing defense focus. A new report from Aerospace Corp. highlights these trends.

title: Artemis 2 countdown underway
summary: The countdown for NASA's Artemis 2 mission around the moon has begun, with no major issues reported for the launch. The two-day countdown started on March 30.

title: Second Starlink satellite suffers anomaly, generating debris
summary: A SpaceX Starlink satellite experienced an anomaly causing debris, marking the second such incident in over three months. The malfunction highlights ongoing concerns about space debris from satellite operations.

title: Rocket Lab wins German approval for Mynaric deal
summary: Rocket Lab has received German regulatory approval for its acquisition of Mynaric, clearing the way for the deal to close in April after months of scrutiny. The decision ensures key space technology remains under domestic control.

title: Varda flies navigation payload, heat shield tests on sixth reentry mission
summary: Varda conducted its sixth reentry mission, testing navigation payloads and heat shield technology. Rhea Space, Sandia, and NASA contributed hardware to gather data during the high-speed descent.

title: ESA launches first Celeste satellites to test complementary LEO navigation layer
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) launched the first two Celeste satellites to test a complementary low Earth orbit (LEO) navigation layer from New Zealand using a Rocket Lab Electron launcher. The satellites were deployed into a quasi-polar LEO at 510 km altitude.

title: SpaceX launches Transporter-16 rideshare mission
summary: SpaceX launched the Transporter-16 rideshare mission on March 30, deploying over 100 payloads to sun-synchronous orbit.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 pilot Victor Glover listens to 'Whitey on the Moon' every Monday. This is why.
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 pilot Victor Glover listens to 'Whitey on the Moon' every Monday, emphasizing its connection to the human condition rather than racism. The song reflects broader themes of struggle and perseverance.

title: Why won't NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts land on the moon when they get there?
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission will send four astronauts on a lunar flyby without landing, as it is a test flight to validate systems for future moon landings. The mission is scheduled for no earlier than April 1.

title: Northern lights could be visible in 16 states tonight March 31 as a speedy CME barrels toward Earth
summary: A coronal mass ejection (CME) is expected to impact Earth, potentially making the northern lights visible in 16 U.S. states, including Alaska and Wyoming, on March 31. The event is caused by a fast-moving solar particle stream interacting with Earth's atmosphere.

title: Huge solar flare no threat to Artemis 2 astronaut launch to the moon, NASA says
summary: A massive X1.4 solar flare will not threaten NASA's Artemis 2 astronaut mission to the moon. NASA confirmed the solar event poses no danger to the crew.

title: Record-breaking 'space laser' erupts from merging galaxies 8 billion light-years away
summary: Astronomers detected the most distant and brightest 'space laser' from merging galaxies 8 billion light-years away, occurring when the universe was half its current age.

title: 25 years ago, 'Invader Zim' arrived, but it was too far ahead of its time
summary: The animated series 'Invader Zim' premiered 25 years ago, noted for its ahead-of-its-time storytelling and humor. The show's dark themes and unique style have since gained a cult following.

title: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS may be nearly 12 billion years old — so ancient its star system may no longer exist
summary: Interstellar comet 3I/ATLAS is estimated to be nearly 12 billion years old, potentially making it as old as the Milky Way itself. Its ancient origin suggests its original star system may no longer exist.

title: Darth Maul was originally going to be the villain of the 'Star Wars' sequels; here's why that would have been a bad idea
summary: Darth Maul was initially considered as the main antagonist for the 'Star Wars' sequel trilogy, but this idea was ultimately abandoned. The decision was made to avoid potential narrative complications and fan expectations.

title: Heading to Florida for NASA's Artemis 2 moon launch? Here's what to know before you go
summary: Florida is preparing for the historic Artemis 2 moon launch, with visitors advised to budget extra time to witness the event. The launch is drawing significant public interest.

title: Best moon movies: 12 lunar adventures to watch ahead of NASA's Artemis 2 launch
summary: An article lists 12 moon-themed movies to watch in anticipation of NASA's upcoming Artemis 2 mission, spanning classics like 'Destination Moon' and 'Apollo 13'.

title: Landsat Reveals Reservoir Changes and Bathymetry
summary: Two recent studies utilized Landsat data to uncover reservoir structure and dynamics, filling critical knowledge gaps. The findings highlight the importance of satellite observations in managing water resources.

title: Sendoff for Artemis II Crew
summary: The Artemis II crew, including NASA astronauts Victor Glover, Christina Koch, Reid Wiseman, and CSA astronauts Jeremy Hansen and Jenni Gibbons, posed for a photo before a media event on March 27, 2026. Douglas and Gibbons are designated as backup crew members.

title: Seeing Blue During Schirmacher’s Summer Melt Season
summary: A network of meltwater lakes and drainage channels has turned the Antarctic ice shelf known for its blue ice areas even bluer during the summer melt season. This phenomenon highlights the effects of climate change on polar regions.

title: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards
summary: Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager, discusses his role in supporting NASA's first crewed Artemis mission to the Moon. His career has focused on enabling spacecraft communication with Earth, inspired by the Space Shuttle Program.

title: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
summary: NASA has selected Intuitive Machines to deliver seven science and technology payloads to the Moon for $180.4 million under the CLPS initiative and Artemis program. The mission aims to enhance understanding of lunar chemical composition and support future human exploration.

title: NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories
summary: NASA announced the categories for its Environment and Energy 'Blue Marble' Awards, recognizing excellence in environmental management, project execution, and sustainability efforts. The awards highlight achievements in compliance, conservation, and stakeholder partnerships.

title: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
summary: The U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR captured an image of Washington's Mount Rainier on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. This marks a significant milestone in Earth observation technology.

title: NISAR Views Mount St. Helens
summary: NISAR, a U.S.-Indian Earth satellite, captured an image of Mount St. Helens on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. The satellite's data provides detailed surface imagery of the Pacific Northwest region.

title: NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch
summary: NASA's technology and science demonstrations will launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the Transporter-16 rideshare mission. These demonstrations aim to test thermal protection systems, advance communications, and deepen understanding of Earth's atmosphere.

title: Launch Preview: SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz launches comprise busy launch manifest
summary: Nine launches are scheduled this week, featuring rockets like SLS, Falcon 9, Atlas V, and Soyuz from locations in Florida, California, and Russia.

title: America’s Rocket Factory comes Full Circle with Artemis
summary: Rocket Factory America is playing a key role in NASA's Artemis program, marking a significant milestone in the return of crewed launches beyond low Earth orbit. The collaboration highlights the growing involvement of private companies in deep-space missions.

title: SpaceX delivers 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit aboard the Transporter 16 mission from California. This marks another milestone in the company's rideshare program.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA revealed detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, marking a significant shift from its previous Gateway-focused lunar program. The agency is now prioritizing a sustainable lunar surface presence over the lunar Gateway station.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider based in Germany, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company had planned to proceed with the launch before calling it off.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, targeting a 2028 launch. The mission was unveiled by Administrator Jared Isaacman and other agency leaders.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is making significant progress on upgrades to its Starbase launch pads in preparation for the upcoming Block 3 Starship launches. This effort highlights SpaceX's focus on increasing launch frequency.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets carrying internet satellites. Multiple countries, including the U.S., Russia, China, New Zealand, and Norway, are preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from the repaired Baikonur Cosmodrome pad. The mission follows the Soyuz MS-28 launch from Site 31/6.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center in space.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned in the context of space industry news, but no specific details are provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of the European Space Agency's contributions to space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly. The mission team reports that the spacecraft is now operational and communicating normally.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting a series of events for industry to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. The events will outline planned activities, thematic priorities, and industrial opportunities across ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato exoplanet-hunting mission is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, will launch on Ariane 6 in January 2027 to monitor over 150,000 stars for Earth-like planets.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical heart. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 software on 30 March 2026, mandating its use by all suppliers for financial proposals in ESA Tender Actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to improve satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky antennas.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for testing robotic and mobility systems. This initiative aims to advance space technology research and education.

title: Voyager and Icarus Team Up on ISS Robotics Demo
summary: Voyager and Icarus collaborate on a robotics demonstration aboard the International Space Station (ISS), marking a significant step in space technology development. This partnership highlights the growing role of private companies in advancing ISS capabilities.

title: Q&A With Lunar Base Manager Carlos Garcia-Galan
summary: Carlos Garcia-Galan transitioned from managing NASA's lunar-orbiting habitat program to overseeing the agency's plan for a lunar surface base. He was interviewed during NASA's Ignition event about his new role.

title: Transporter-16 Sends 119 Payloads to Orbit
summary: Transporter-16 successfully launched 119 payloads to orbit. This marks another cargo mission under the rideshare program.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M Series A at $1.1B Valuation
summary: Starcloud secured $170 million in Series A funding at a $1.1 billion valuation, highlighting investor demand for orbital data center ventures. The company stands out as a data-center startup with proven flight heritage.

title: Bellatrix Raises $20M For In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million for in-space propulsion technology. The funding will support scaling production infrastructure after flight-qualifying core technologies.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems has raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS network in Low Earth Orbit (LEO). The company aims to provide Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) signals that are stronger, more accurate, and more secure than traditional GPS.

title: Jared Isaacman Hits 100 Days In Office With a Bang
summary: Jared Isaacman, NASA Administrator, marks 100 days in office with a bold announcement about returning to the Moon. The statement emphasizes an accelerated timeline for lunar missions.

title: With Artemis Changes, Europe is Left Holding the Bag
summary: ESA and European space companies invested heavily in components for the lunar orbiting station under Artemis plans, but recent changes have left them in a difficult position. Europe now faces uncertainty regarding its role and contributions.

title: Arkadia Space Secures €14.5M to Develop New Biprop Engine
summary: Arkadia Space, a Spanish propulsion startup, secured €14.5 million from the European Investment Council to develop a new bipropellant engine.

title: Lawmakers Question NASA’s Shifting Vision for CLDs
summary: Lawmakers are questioning NASA's evolving vision for Commercial Low Earth Orbit Destinations (CLDs), as proposed by Administrator Jared Isaacman. The new plan has raised concerns and confusion among legislators regarding its direction and feasibility.

title: Starcloud Raises $170M to Fund Orbital Data Center Plans
summary: Starcloud, a space startup, raised $170 million in a Series A funding round to develop orbital data centers using satellites. The company is now valued at $1.1 billion.

title: WAVE Achieves First Cloud-to-Gateway Satcom Virtualization with AI Signal Analysis
summary: The WAVE Consortium, an IEEE standards body, achieved the first standardized waveform virtualization using FPGA acceleration in a cloud-to-gateway satellite communication (Satcom) system with AI signal analysis.

title: SpaceX Launches 119 Payloads on the Transporter-16 Rideshare Mission
summary: SpaceX successfully launched 119 payloads into Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) on the Transporter-16 rideshare mission from Vandenberg Space Force Base. This dedicated smallsat mission marks another milestone in SpaceX's rideshare program.

title: Live in the Booth: AST SpaceMobile President Scott Wisniewski Talks Spectrum Strategy and Defense Potential
summary: AST SpaceMobile President Scott Wisniewski discusses the company's spectrum strategy and defense potential during SATShow Week, highlighting the recent launch of the BlueBird 6 satellite.

title: SpaceBridge Launches UniHub as Streamlined VSAT Platform
summary: SpaceBridge launched UniHub, a compact all-in-one VSAT hub platform designed to simplify satellite network deployment with high performance, efficiency, and scalability.

title: Bridgit Mendler Outlines Northwood’s Vision for Rapidly Deployable Ground Infrastructure
summary: Bridgit Mendler discussed Northwood Space's efforts to address satellite ground infrastructure challenges by creating a deployable end-to-end ground system. The initiative aims to streamline satellite operations with scalable and efficient infrastructure solutions.

title: Interview With AvL CTO Chris Rose at SATShow Week
summary: AvL Technologies CTO Chris Rose discusses the evolution of tactical fly-away systems during an interview at SATShow Week. The conversation highlights advancements in this technology from AvL's booth.

title: D2D, Sovereign Demand, and Mega-Valuations: Space Leaders Navigate Market Volatility
summary: Space industry leaders at SATShow expressed uncertainty about the market's outlook due to volatility. The discussion focused on D2D (Direct-to-Device) services, sovereign demand, and high valuations.

title: Interview With Qorvo Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings at SATShow Week
summary: Qorvo's Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings discusses the company's RF solutions, including phased arrays for LEO satellites, during SATShow Week. The interview highlights advancements in satellite communication technology.

title: Sovereign Satcom Networks Grapple With Data Security Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
summary: Nations are strengthening control over their communications architectures due to geopolitical uncertainty, raising questions about the definition and capabilities of sovereign space infrastructure.
